original_hypothesis.title: THORChain_Aggregator rescueFunds Enables Owner Extraction of ERC20s Stuck from Failed V5 Swaps
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When _transferOutAndCallV5 (ERC20 path) sends tokens to the aggregator and the aggregator's swapOutV5 fails, the Router does not revert (by design). Tokens are permanently stuck in the aggregator. The THORChain_Aggregator.rescueFunds function, callable only by the owner, can extract these tokens. A malicious or compromised aggregator owner can collect all ERC20 tokens from failed V5 outbound swaps.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This combines two documented issues: (1) ERC20 tokens stuck on aggregator swap failure with no recovery, and (2) owner-only rescueFunds that can extract any asset. The owner has exclusive access to tokens that belong to protocol users.

proof_of_signal.pattern: onlyOwner rescueFunds can extract any token; no mechanism to return stuck tokens to original recipients

preconditions: Aggregator owner is malicious or compromised
ERC20 _transferOutAndCallV5 swap has failed, leaving tokens in aggregator
No alternative recovery mechanism for affected users

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: rescueFunds(asset, amount, destination) with no user-facing recovery
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Owner calls rescueFunds to extract stuck ERC20s to their own address
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Profit = total value of ERC20s stuck from failed swaps

economic_questions: How frequently do V5 ERC20 aggregator swaps fail?
What is the expected cumulative value of stuck tokens?
Is there any off-chain mechanism for users to reclaim stuck tokens?
validation_requirements: Confirm ERC20 tokens remain in aggregator after swapOutV5 failure
Verify rescueFunds can extract arbitrary amounts to arbitrary addresses
Check if aggregator owner is a multisig or EOA

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After any failed V5 ERC20 swap
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Trust violation; user funds at owner's discretion

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Users whose V5 ERC20 swaps failed at aggregator
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Full value of their failed swap amount
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Cumulative value of all failed V5 ERC20 swaps

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Aggregator owner address and governance structure
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Trace full lifecycle of failed ERC20 V5 swap to stuck state

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
